Text is the primary way in which you represent knowledge in SuperMemo. You will spend a lot of time processing texts, esp. if you learn to employ [[Glossary:Incremental_reading|incremental reading]]. This is why your decisions about choosing appropriate text [[Glossary:Component|components]] are important. 

Plain text [[Glossary:Component|components]] have traditionally been the most often used text components in earlier versions of SuperMemo. They consume little space and can be searched at high speeds with SuperMemo low-level search procedures. With the advent of [[Glossary:Incremental_reading|incremental reading]], however, [[HTML component|HTML components]] have become the default text [[Glossary:Component|components]] in SuperMemo. [[HTML component|HTML components]] provide many formatting options, however, they consume more disk space. If disk space is very important for you, use ''Classic'' [[Glossary:Template|template]] (and its derivatives) for [[Glossary:Item|items]] instead of HTML-based templates (see: [[Templates|Using templates]]). Optimally, you should use [[HTML component|HTML components]] with [[Glossary:Incremental_reading|incremental reading]] and use ''Classic'' plain text [[Glossary:Template|templates]] on [[Glossary:Item|items]] that have been completed (i.e. formulated in their final shape). SuperMemo automatically removes formatting from texts used in plain text [[Glossary:Component|components]] when you run '''[[File menu|File]] : [[Repairing collections|Repair collection]]''' with '''Rebuild registries''' checked. This practice helps you keep your [[Glossary:Collection|collections]] compact and fast. 

The table below summarizes pros and cons of using various text [[Glossary:Component|components]] in SuperMemo: 

{| style="background-color: rgb(192, 192, 192);" border="1" cellspacing="5" cellpadding="5"
|-
| align="center" | '''Type''' 
| align="center" | '''Advantages''' 
| align="center" | '''Disadvantages''' 
| align="center" | '''Typical use'''
|-
| [[HTML component|'''HTML''' component]] 
This [[Glossary:Component|component]] is used by [[Glossary:Template|templates]] such as ''Item'', ''Article'', ''Item Picture'', etc. 

| 
#richest formatting 
#full incremental reading support 
#full HTML and JavaScript support 
#images can be inserted in the text 
#Unicode support 
#search possible 
#search&replace possible 
#phonetic transcription possible 
#can use stylesheets

| 
#Internet Explorer 6.0 or later required 
#fonts can be modified by [[Glossary:Template|templates]] only before the text is typed in (unless you use a stylesheet with the template) 
#all texts kept in separate files (this slows down copying of large [[Glossary:Collection|collections]])

| 
#default text format 
#[[Glossary:Incremental_reading|incremental reading]]
#richly formatted files with scripts and multimedia 
#whole websites 
#files that can be displayed in Internet Explorer (e.g. XLS, PDF, DOC, RTF, GIF, etc.)

|-
| [[WebView component|'''WebView''' component]]
This [[Glossary:Component|component]] uses a modern browser engine (e.g. Chromium-based WebView) to display HTML content.

|
#modern HTML, CSS, and JavaScript support
#renders pages more accurately than Internet Explorer
#better security and stability
#full Unicode support
#fast rendering of large documents
#supports modern web standards (HTML5, CSS3)
#search possible
#search&replace possible

|
#may not support all legacy Internet Explorer features
#slightly higher memory use than the classic HTML component
#some older scripts or templates may require adaptation
#[[incremental reading]] support is rudimentary

|
#modern HTML content
#web pages requiring up‑to‑date browser standards
#items using interactive JavaScript widgets
#incremental reading when modern rendering is preferred

|-
| '''Plain text''' component 
This [[Glossary:Component|component]] is used by the ''Classic'' template 

| 
#fonts can be modified globally via [[Glossary:Template|templates]]
#small size of data 
#speed 
#can be made transparent (e.g. to write over images) 
#search possible 
#search&replace possible (including [[Filter files|'''Apply filter''']]) 
#automatic translation and phonetic transcription

| 
#no rich formatting 
#unsuitable for [[Glossary:Incremental_reading|incremental reading]]

| 
#simple question-answer [[Glossary:Item|items]] (e.g. created with '''Remember cloze''') 
#transparent labels

|-
| '''Rich text (RTF)''' component 
This [[Glossary:Component|component]] is used in templates such as ''RTF Article'', ''RTF Item'', etc. 

| 
#rich text formatting 
#subset of [[Glossary:Incremental_reading|incremental reading]]
#search possible 
#search&replace possible 
#phonetic transcription possible

| 
#may not be supported in the future 
#large size of files 
#very slow processing of longer texts or large [[Glossary:Collection|collections]]
#cannot modify font with [[Glossary:Template|templates]] nor stylesheets 
#cannot interlace pictures and other multimedia

| 
#[[Glossary:Incremental_reading|incremental reading]] in the absence of Internet Explorer 
#rich formatting in the absence of Internet Explorer

|-
| '''Spelling''' [[Glossary:Component|component]]
Used in the [[Glossary:Template|template]] ''Spelling'' 

| 
#used for spelling tests 
#small size 
#speed 
#font modifiable via [[Glossary:Template|templates]]
#search possible 
#search&replace possible

| 
#useless beyond spelling tests

| 
#spelling exercises

|-
| Binary [[Glossary:Component|component]]
| 
#using all possible proprietary formats that are not supported by other [[Glossary:Component|components]]

| 
#clicking the '''Run''' button is necessary to view the text 
#files can only be edited if the proprietary editor is installed 
#words do not enter the lexicon 
#search not possible 
#search&replace not possible 
#translation and phonetic transcription not available 
#cannot modify font via [[Glossary:Template|templates]]

| 
#importing all text formats that are not supported by the [[HTML component]] (e.g. mind maps, etc.)

|-
| [[OLE component|'''OLE''' component]] 
| 
#using proprietary formats that support OLE (e.g. mind maps)

| 
#requires a relevant OLE server 
#very large size of files 
#files can only be edited if the proprietary editor is installed 
#words do not enter the lexicon 
#search not possible 
#search&replace not possible 
#translation and phonetic transcription not available 
#may be annoyingly unstable if there are problems with the OLE server 
#cannot modify font via [[Glossary:Template|templates]]

| 
#using data formats that cannot be used with [[HTML component]] but support OLE in the presence of an OLE server

|-
| '''Sound''' [[Glossary:Component|component]] 
| 
#sound can be associated with the text 
#search possible 
#search&replace possible (including [[Filter files|'''Apply filter''']]) 
#font modifiable via [[Glossary:Template|templates]] 
#phonetic transcription and translation supported

| 
#slow 
#cannot be made transparent 
#provides no advantages over plain text if no sound is associated

| 
#pronunciation exercises 
#learning material narration

|-
| '''Image''' [[Glossary:Component|component]] 
| 
#can represent most complex formatting needed for mathematical or chemical formulas (as a picture, e.g. JPG)

| 
#large size of files 
#words do not enter the lexicon 
#search not possible 
#search&replace not possible 
#translation and phonetic transcription not available 
#cannot modify font via [[Glossary:Template|templates]]

| 
#complex formulas that are hard to represent in HTML or OLE

|}


== See also ==

*[https://super-memory.com/help/faq/texts.htm '''FAQ''': Text components] 
*[[Using fonts]] 
*[[Fonts tab in Options|Font options]] 
*[https://supermemopedia.com/wiki/Finding_out_the_default_font_size_used_in_HTML_components#Followup FAQ: Why so many complex components?] 
*[https://supermemopedia.com/wiki/How_can_I_see_if_a_component_is_plain_or_HTML%3F How to differentiate between plain text and HTML?]
